_comment: Canonical cross-surface facts for the GeoLens README, marketing site (getgeolens.com/src), and docs site (getgeolens.com/docs). Validated against scripts/install.sh + backend/pyproject.toml by scripts/check_docs_contract.py (geolens CI). The getgeolens.com repo vendors a copy and enforces the same `forbidden` list in its own CI. See docs-internal/DOC-CONSISTENCY-STRATEGY for the full design. Edit a fact ONCE here (and in its canonical source) — the gates make every surface follow.

claims.kubernetes: Docker Compose is the supported path; an experimental community-maintained Helm chart is available for Kubernetes
claims.egress: no product telemetry; outbound calls only from integrations you enable

forbidden.reason: no such compose file — use docker-compose.yml / docker-compose.prod.yml
raw API port + missing /api — use http://localhost:8080/api/collections
no public conformance certification — say 'OGC API' / 'implements OGC API'
no public conformance certification — say 'OGC API'
say 'OGC API support' (marketing) or 'OGC API conformance' (standards)
the admin password is auto-generated at install — never admin/admin
